sourceMovie.title: Bride Wars
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Bride Wars (2009) as a comedy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The comedy and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Forrest Gump
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Bride Wars clicked for you, Forrest Gump (1994) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Bride Wars?
faq.answer: Try Forrest Gump, (500) Days of Summer, 27 Dresses—each shares DNA with Bride Wars while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Bride Wars?
faq.answer: Bride Wars (2009) is best described as Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Bride Wars worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
